summary: Smoking China Rider, soaring Playin’ in the Band, killer Deep Elem
blurb: The show delivers a smoking China Rider and a soaring Playin' In The Band jam, with a tight first‑set capped by Bertha. Jerry’s guitar shines on the China Cat > I Know You Rider transition while Brent adds jazzy space. Energy stays high despite a few uneven moments early in the set.
review: April 14, 1982 at Glenns Falls Civic Center finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. The first set opens with a gritty Jack Straw and rolls into a deep‑blues showcase of Deep Elem Blues that many reviewers call “superb.” By the second set the band is locked in – the classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue feels electric, and Playin' In The Band erupts into a soaring, space‑filled marathon that carries you through drums, space, The Wheel, and back into a second explosive Playing In The Band before closing with Johnny B. Goode and U.S. Blues. Jerry’s tone is aggressive yet fluid, especially on the China Rider/China Cat moments, while Phil’s bass drives the jams forward. Brent’s keyboards are loud and jazzy, adding a dreamy “space” segment that lifts the second‑set energy. Bob Weir pushes through a cold but still delivers spirited vocals. The crowd’s enthusiasm is palpable, making this one of the more memorable early‑'80 shows despite a few vocal mix quirks in the first set.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings (4 positive, 1 mixed, 1 negative) consistently praise the performance – standout China Rider/China Cat, Playin' In The Band jam, Deep Elem Blues and tight rhythm section. No recurring criticism of playing quality, only minor mix issues, leading to a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, clear mix; highlights China Rider, Playin' In The Band and Jack Straw without major drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Smoking China Rider (China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider) that electrifies the second set
Soaring Playin' In The Band jam with jazzy Brent space segment
Killer Deep Elem Blues performance early in the first set
Tight rhythm and bass driving the energy throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Aggressive yet fluid guitar work shines, especially on the China Rider/China Cat transition and the soaring Playin' In The Band jam.
band_performance.Phil: Driving bass provides a solid foundation; no criticisms noted.
band_performance.Bob: Energetic rhythm guitar and vocals despite a cold, with a humorous opening line that adds fun to the set.
band_performance.Brent: Busy, loud keyboards add jazzy space during Playin' In The Band; mix is turned up but effective.
